<content>1: """
2: Migration Compatibility Tests
3: 
4: This module tests compatibility after database migrations for:
5: 1. Entry.objects.filter(user=user) - Basic ORM operations work after migrations
6: 2. search_with_embeddings() - Vector search still works
7: 3. FTS search - Full-text search works with search_vector field
8: 4. Agent isolation - User data remains isolated after migrations
9: 
10: Test coverage for migrations:
11: - 0100_add_search_vector.py - Adds search_vector field and GIN index
12: - 0101_add_context_summary.py - Adds context_summary field
13: - 0102_add_chunk_scale.py - Adds chunk_scale field
14: """
15: 
16: import math
17: from unittest.mock import patch
18: 
19: import pytest
20: import torch
21: from asgiref.sync import sync_to_async
22: from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
23: from django.db.models import Q
24: 
25: from khoj.database.adapters import EntryAdapters
26: from khoj.database.models import Agent, Entry, KhojUser, SearchModelConfig
27: from tests.helpers import ChatModelFactory, SearchModelFactory, UserFactory
28: 
29:

34:     user2 = UserFactory(username="test_user_2", email="user2@test.com")
35:     user3 = UserFactory(username="test_user_3", email="user3@test.com")
36:     return user1, user2, user3

52:         personality="Test personality",
53:     )

69: class TestEntryOrmOperationsAfterMigrations:
70:     """Test basic ORM operations on Entry model after migrations."""
71: 
72:     @pytest.mark.django_db
73:     def test_create_entry_with_all_new_fields(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings):
74:         """Create Entry with all new fields and verify data integrity."""
75:         user = test_users[0]
76: 
77:         entry = Entry.objects.create(
78:             user=user,
79:             embeddings=sample_embeddings,
80:             raw="Test raw content",
81:             compiled="Test compiled content",
82:             heading="Test Heading",
83:             file_type=Entry.EntryType.PLAINTEXT,
84:             file_path="/test/path/file.txt",
85:             file_name="file.txt",
86:             hashed_value="test_hash_123",
87:             search_model=search_model,
88:             # New fields from migrations 0100-0102
89:             search_vector=SearchVector("compiled"),
90:             context_summary="Test context summary",
91:             chunk_scale="1024",
92:         )
93: 
94:         # Verify entry was created
95:         assert entry.id is not None
96:         assert entry.user == user
97:         assert entry.compiled == "Test compiled content"
98: 
99:         # Verify new fields are set
100:         assert entry.context_summary == "Test context summary"
101:         assert entry.chunk_scale == "1024"
102: 
103:     @pytest.mark.django_db
104:     def test_filter_by_user(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
105:         """Filter entries by user and verify data integrity."""
106:         user1, user2, _ = test_users
107: 
108:         # Create entries for user1
109:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list[:3]):
110:             Entry.objects.create(
111:                 user=user1,
112:                 embeddings=emb,
113:                 raw=f"User1 content {i}",
114:                 compiled=f"User1 compiled {i}",
115:                 hashed_value=f"hash_user1_{i}",
116:                 search_model=search_model,
117:                 chunk_scale="512",
118:             )
119: 
120:         # Create entries for user2
121:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list[3:]):
122:             Entry.objects.create(
123:                 user=user2,
124:                 embeddings=emb,
125:                 raw=f"User2 content {i}",
126:                 compiled=f"User2 compiled {i}",
127:                 hashed_value=f"hash_user2_{i}",
128:                 search_model=search_model,
129:                 chunk_scale="1024",
130:             )
131: 
132:         # Filter by user1
133:         user1_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=user1)
134:         assert user1_entries.count() == 3
135: 
136:         # Filter by user2
137:         user2_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=user2)
138:         assert user2_entries.count() == 2
139: 
140:         # Verify content integrity
141:         for entry in user1_entries:
142:             assert entry.user == user1
143:             assert "User1" in entry.raw
144: 
145:         for entry in user2_entries:
146:             assert entry.user == user2
147:             assert "User2" in entry.raw
148: 
149:     @pytest.mark.django_db
150:     def test_entry_queryset_chaining(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
151:         """Test that ORM queryset chaining works correctly after migrations."""
152:         user = test_users[0]
153: 
154:         # Create entries with different file types
155:         file_types = [
156:             Entry.EntryType.PLAINTEXT,
157:             Entry.EntryType.MARKDOWN,
158:             Entry.EntryType.ORG,
159:         ]
160: 
161:         for i, (emb, file_type) in enumerate(zip(sample_embeddings_list[:3], file_types)):
162:             Entry.objects.create(
163:                 user=user,
164:                 embeddings=emb,
165:                 raw=f"Content {i}",
166:                 compiled=f"Compiled {i}",
167:                 file_type=file_type,
168:                 hashed_value=f"hash_{i}",
169:                 search_model=search_model,
170:                 chunk_scale=str(512 * (i + 1)),
171:             )
172: 
173:         # Test queryset chaining with new fields
174:         results = (
175:             Entry.objects.filter(user=user)
176:             .exclude(file_type=Entry.EntryType.IMAGE)
177:             .filter(chunk_scale__in=["512", "1024"])
178:             .order_by("-created_at")
179:         )
180: 
181:         assert results.count() == 2

184: class TestSearchWithEmbeddingsAfterMigrations:
185:     """Test vector similarity search after migrations."""
186: 
187:     @pytest.mark.django_db
188:     def test_search_with_embeddings_basic(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
189:         """Perform vector similarity search and verify search works."""
190:         user = test_users[0]
191: 
192:         # Create entries with different embeddings
193:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list):
194:             Entry.objects.create(
195:                 user=user,
196:                 embeddings=emb,
197:                 raw=f"Test content about topic {i}",
198:                 compiled=f"Compiled content about topic {i}",
199:                 hashed_value=f"hash_{i}",
200:                 search_model=search_model,
201:                 chunk_scale="1024",
202:             )
203: 
204:         # Create a query embedding
205:         query_embedding = torch.randn(384)
206: 
207:         # Perform search
208:         results = EntryAdapters.search_with_embeddings(
209:             raw_query="topic",
210:             embeddings=query_embedding,
211:             user=user,
212:             max_results=3,
213:         )
214: 
215:         # Verify search returns results
216:         assert results is not None
217:         # Note: Actual similarity values depend on random embeddings
218:         assert len(list(results)) <= 3
219: 
220:     @pytest.mark.django_db
221:     def test_search_with_embeddings_with_agent(self, test_users, test_agent, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
222:         """Test vector search with agent filter."""
223:         user = test_users[0]
224: 
225:         # Create entries for user
226:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list[:2]):
227:             Entry.objects.create(
228:                 user=user,
229:                 embeddings=emb,
230:                 raw=f"User content {i}",
231:                 compiled=f"User compiled {i}",
232:                 hashed_value=f"user_hash_{i}",
233:                 search_model=search_model,
234:             )
235: 
236:         # Create entries for agent
237:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list[2:]):
238:             Entry.objects.create(
239:                 agent=test_agent,
240:                 embeddings=emb,
241:                 raw=f"Agent content {i}",
242:                 compiled=f"Agent compiled {i}",
243:                 hashed_value=f"agent_hash_{i}",
244:                 search_model=search_model,
245:             )
246: 
247:         # Search with user and agent
248:         query_embedding = torch.randn(384)
249:         results = EntryAdapters.search_with_embeddings(
250:             raw_query="content",
251:             embeddings=query_embedding,
252:             user=user,
253:             agent=test_agent,
254:             max_results=10,
255:         )
256: 
257:         # Should find both user and agent entries
258:         assert results is not None
259: 
260:     @pytest.mark.django_db
261:     def test_search_with_file_type_filter(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
262:         """Test vector search with file type filter after migrations."""
263:         user = test_users[0]
264: 
265:         # Create entries with different file types
266:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list):

268:             Entry.objects.create(

270:                 embeddings=emb,

274:                 hashed_value=f"hash_{i}",
275:                 search_model=search_model,
276:                 chunk_scale="512" if i % 2 == 0 else "1024",

281:         results = EntryAdapters.search_with_embeddings(
282:             raw_query="content",

284:             user=user,
285:             file_type_filter=Entry.EntryType.PLAINTEXT,
286:             max_results=10,
287:         )
288: 
289:         # Verify only plaintext results
290:         for result in results:
291:             assert result.file_type == Entry.EntryType.PLAINTEXT

294: class TestFtsSearchAfterMigrations:
295:     """Test full-text search with search_vector field after migrations."""
296: 
297:     @pytest.mark.django_db
298:     def test_search_vector_field_operations(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings):
299:         """Test search_vector field operations and GIN index usage."""
300:         user = test_users[0]
301: 
302:         # Create entry with search_vector
303:         entry = Entry.objects.create(
304:             user=user,
305:             embeddings=sample_embeddings,
306:             raw="PostgreSQL full-text search testing",
307:             compiled="Testing PostgreSQL full-text search capabilities",
308:             hashed_value="hash_fts_1",
309:             search_model=search_model,
310:         )
311: 
312:         # Update search_vector
313:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(
314:             search_vector=SearchVector("compiled", "raw")
315:         )
316: 
317:         # Retrieve and verify
318:         entry.refresh_from_db()
319:         assert entry.search_vector is not None
320: 
321:     @pytest.mark.django_db
322:     def test_fts_query_with_search_vector(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
323:         """Perform FTS query and verify results."""
324:         user = test_users[0]
325: 
326:         # Create entries with different content
327:         contents = [
328:             "Python programming tutorial",
329:             "Django web framework guide",
330:             "PostgreSQL database administration",
331:             "Machine learning with Python",
332:         ]
333: 
334:         for i, (emb, content) in enumerate(zip(sample_embeddings_list, contents)):
335:             entry = Entry.objects.create(
336:                 user=user,
337:                 embeddings=emb,
338:                 raw=content,
339:                 compiled=f"Compiled: {content}",
340:                 hashed_value=f"hash_fts_{i}",
341:                 search_model=search_model,
342:             )
343:             # Set search_vector
344:             Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(
345:                 search_vector=SearchVector("raw", "compiled")
346:             )
347: 
348:         # Search using search_vector
349:         results = Entry.objects.filter(
350:             user=user,
351:             search_vector=SearchVector("raw").__eq__("Python"),
352:         )
353: 
354:         # Should find entries containing "Python"
355:         found_titles = [r.raw for r in results]
356:         assert any("Python" in title for title in found_titles)
357: 
358:     @pytest.mark.django_db
359:     def test_gin_index_exists(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings):
360:         """Verify GIN index on search_vector field is being used."""
361:         from django.db import connection
362: 
363:         user = test_users[0]
364: 
365:         # Create an entry
366:         entry = Entry.objects.create(
367:             user=user,
368:             embeddings=sample_embeddings,
369:             raw="Test content for GIN index",
370:             compiled="Compiled test content",
371:             hashed_value="hash_gin",
372:             search_model=search_model,
373:         )
374: 
375:         # Update search_vector
376:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(
377:             search_vector=SearchVector("raw")
378:         )
379: 
380:         # Check if GIN index exists by querying pg_indexes
381:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:
382:             cursor.execute("""
383:                 SELECT indexname
384:                 FROM pg_indexes
385:                 WHERE tablename = 'database_entry'
386:                 AND indexname LIKE '%search_vector%gin%'
387:             """)
388:             result = cursor.fetchone()
389: 
390:         # The GIN index should exist (added in migration 0100)
391:         assert result is not None, "GIN index on search_vector not found"
392:

394: class TestUserIsolationAfterMigrations:
395:     """Test that user data remains isolated after migrations."""
396: 
397:     @pytest.mark.django_db
398:     def test_user_entry_isolation(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
399:         """Verify users can only access their own entries."""
400:         user1, user2, user3 = test_users
401: 
402:         # Create entries for each user with new fields
403:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list):
404:             Entry.objects.create(
405:                 user=user1,
406:                 embeddings=emb,
407:                 raw=f"User1 private content {i}",
408:                 compiled=f"User1 compiled {i}",
409:                 hashed_value=f"hash_u1_{i}",
410:                 search_model=search_model,
411:                 context_summary=f"User1 summary {i}",
412:                 chunk_scale="512",
413:             )
414: 
415:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list):
416:             Entry.objects.create(
417:                 user=user2,
418:                 embeddings=emb,
419:                 raw=f"User2 private content {i}",
420:                 compiled=f"User2 compiled {i}",
421:                 hashed_value=f"hash_u2_{i}",
422:                 search_model=search_model,
423:                 context_summary=f"User2 summary {i}",
424:                 chunk_scale="1024",
425:             )
426: 
427:         # Verify user1 can only see their own entries
428:         user1_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=user1)
429:         assert user1_entries.count() == 5
430:         for entry in user1_entries:
431:             assert entry.user == user1
432:             assert "User1" in entry.raw
433: 
434:         # Verify user2 can only see their own entries
435:         user2_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=user2)
436:         assert user2_entries.count() == 5
437:         for entry in user2_entries:
438:             assert entry.user == user2
439:             assert "User2" in entry.raw
440: 
441:         # Verify user3 has no entries
442:         user3_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=user3)
443:         assert user3_entries.count() == 0
444: 
445:     @pytest.mark.django_db
446:     def test_cross_user_search_isolation(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
447:         """Test that search respects user boundaries with new fields."""
448:         user1, user2, _ = test_users
449: 
450:         # Create entries for both users
451:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list[:2]):
452:             Entry.objects.create(
453:                 user=user1,
454:                 embeddings=emb,
455:                 raw=f"Shared keyword content for user1",
456:                 compiled=f"User1 compiled",
457:                 hashed_value=f"hash_iso1_{i}",
458:                 search_model=search_model,
459:                 chunk_scale="512",
460:             )
461: 
462:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list[2:4]):
463:             Entry.objects.create(
464:                 user=user2,
465:                 embeddings=emb,
466:                 raw=f"Shared keyword content for user2",
467:                 compiled=f"User2 compiled",
468:                 hashed_value=f"hash_iso2_{i}",
469:                 search_model=search_model,
470:                 chunk_scale="1024",
471:             )
472: 
473:         # Search as user1
474:         query_embedding = torch.randn(384)
475:         user1_results = EntryAdapters.search_with_embeddings(
476:             raw_query="Shared keyword",
477:             embeddings=query_embedding,
478:             user=user1,
479:             max_results=10,
480:         )
481: 
482:         # Verify all results belong to user1
483:         for result in user1_results:
484:             assert result.user == user1
485: 
486:         # Search as user2
487:         user2_results = EntryAdapters.search_with_embeddings(
488:             raw_query="Shared keyword",
489:             embeddings=query_embedding,
490:             user=user2,
491:             max_results=10,
492:         )
493: 
494:         # Verify all results belong to user2
495:         for result in user2_results:
496:             assert result.user == user2

499: class TestNewFieldsNullable:
500:     """Test that new fields allow NULL values as specified."""
501: 
502:     @pytest.mark.django_db
503:     def test_context_summary_nullable(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings):
504:         """Verify context_summary allows NULL."""
505:         user = test_users[0]
506: 
507:         entry = Entry.objects.create(
508:             user=user,
509:             embeddings=sample_embeddings,
510:             raw="Test content",
511:             compiled="Test compiled",
512:             hashed_value="hash_null_ctx",
513:             search_model=search_model,
514:             # context_summary is not set - should be NULL
515:         )
516: 
517:         entry.refresh_from_db()
518:         assert entry.context_summary is None or entry.context_summary == ""
519: 
520:     @pytest.mark.django_db

522:         """Verify chunk_scale allows NULL."""
523:         user = test_users[0]
524: 
525:         entry = Entry.objects.create(
526:             user=user,
527:             embeddings=sample_embeddings,
528:             raw="Test content",
529:             compiled="Test compiled",
530:             hashed_value="hash_null_chunk",
531:             search_model=search_model,
532:             # chunk_scale is not set - should use default or be NULL
533:         )

558: class TestBackwardCompatibility:
559:     """Test backward compatibility with entries without new fields."""
560: 
561:     @pytest.mark.django_db
562:     def test_create_entry_without_new_fields(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings):
563:         """Create entries without new fields (as if old code) and verify defaults."""
564:         user = test_users[0]
565: 
566:         # Simulate old code creating entry without new fields
567:         entry = Entry.objects.create(
568:             user=user,
569:             embeddings=sample_embeddings,
570:             raw="Old style entry",
571:             compiled="Old style compiled",
572:             hashed_value="hash_old",
573:             search_model=search_model,
574:             # Not setting: search_vector, context_summary, chunk_scale
575:         )
576: 
577:         entry.refresh_from_db()
578: 
579:         # Verify entry exists
580:         assert entry.id is not None
581: 
582:         # Verify new fields have appropriate default values
583:         assert entry.search_vector is None  # NULL is allowed
584:         assert entry.context_summary is None  # NULL is allowed
585:         assert entry.chunk_scale == "default"  # Has default value
586: 
587:     @pytest.mark.django_db
588:     def test_query_entries_mixed_new_and_old(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
589:         """Query entries with mixed presence of new fields."""
590:         user = test_users[0]
591: 
592:         # Create old-style entry
593:         old_entry = Entry.objects.create(
594:             user=user,
595:             embeddings=sample_embeddings_list[0],
596:             raw="Old entry",
597:             compiled="Old compiled",
598:             hashed_value="hash_old_mixed",
599:             search_model=search_model,
600:         )
601: 
602:         # Create new-style entry
603:         new_entry = Entry.objects.create(
604:             user=user,
605:             embeddings=sample_embeddings_list[1],
606:             raw="New entry",
607:             compiled="New compiled",
608:             hashed_value="hash_new_mixed",
609:             search_model=search_model,
610:             context_summary="New context summary",
611:             chunk_scale="2048",
612:         )
613: 
614:         # Query all entries - should work
615:         all_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=user)
616:         assert all_entries.count() == 2
617: 
618:         # Verify both entries are accessible
619:         entry_ids = set(e.id for e in all_entries)
620:         assert old_entry.id in entry_ids
621:         assert new_entry.id in entry_ids
622: 
623:     @pytest.mark.django_db
624:     def test_filter_by_new_fields_with_nulls(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
625:         """Filter by new fields when some entries have NULL values."""
626:         user = test_users[0]
627: 
628:         # Create entries with and without context_summary
629:         Entry.objects.create(
630:             user=user,
631:             embeddings=sample_embeddings_list[0],
632:             raw="Entry without summary",
633:             compiled="Compiled without summary",
634:             hashed_value="hash_no_summary",
635:             search_model=search_model,
636:         )
637: 
638:         Entry.objects.create(
639:             user=user,
640:             embeddings=sample_embeddings_list[1],
641:             raw="Entry with summary",
642:             compiled="Compiled with summary",
643:             hashed_value="hash_with_summary",
644:             search_model=search_model,
645:             context_summary="This entry has a summary",
646:         )
647: 
648:         # Filter for entries with context_summary
649:         with_summary = Entry.objects.filter(
650:             user=user,
651:             context_summary__isnull=False,
652:         )
653:         assert with_summary.count() == 1

658:             user=user,
659:             context_summary__isnull=True,
660:         )
661:         assert without_summary.count() == 1

910: class TestMultiScaleChunkingIntegration:
911:     """Test multi-scale chunking integration with new fields."""
912: 
913:     @pytest.mark.django_db
914:     def test_multi_scale_entries_coexist(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
915:         """Test that entries with different chunk scales can coexist."""
916:         user = test_users[0]
917: 
918:         # Create entries representing different chunk scales of the same content
919:         base_content = "This is a test document for multi-scale chunking."
920: 
921:         scales = ["512", "1024", "2048"]
922:         entries = []
923: 
924:         for i, (emb, scale) in enumerate(zip(sample_embeddings_list[:3], scales)):
925:             entry = Entry.objects.create(
926:                 user=user,
927:                 embeddings=emb,
928:                 raw=f"{base_content} Scale: {scale}",
929:                 compiled=f"Compiled scale {scale}",
930:                 hashed_value=f"hash_multiscale_{i}",
931:                 search_model=search_model,
932:                 chunk_scale=scale,
933:                 context_summary=f"Summary for {scale} chunk",
934:             )
935:             entries.append(entry)
936: 
937:         # Verify all entries exist
938:         assert len(entries) == 3
939: 
940:         # Verify each has correct scale
941:         for entry in entries:
942:             assert entry.chunk_scale in scales
943:             assert entry.context_summary.startswith("Summary for")
944: 
945:     @pytest.mark.django_db
946:     def test_search_across_chunk_scales(self, test_users, search_model, sample_embeddings_list):
947:         """Test search works across entries with different chunk scales."""
948:         user = test_users[0]
949: 
950:         # Create entries with different chunk scales
951:         for i, emb in enumerate(sample_embeddings_list):
952:             Entry.objects.create(
953:                 user=user,
954:                 embeddings=emb,
955:                 raw=f"Multi-scale content test",
956:                 compiled=f"Compiled content for scale",
957:                 hashed_value=f"hash_search_ms_{i}",
958:                 search_model=search_model,
959:                 chunk_scale=["512", "1024", "2048"][i % 3],
960:             )
961: 
962:         # Search across all scales
963:         query_embedding = torch.randn(384)
964:         results = EntryAdapters.search_with_embeddings(
965:             raw_query="multi-scale content",
966:             embeddings=query_embedding,
967:             user=user,
968:             max_results=10,
969:         )
970: 
971:         # Should find results regardless of chunk scale
972:         assert results is not None
